Speaker 1: During this video, I'm going to be talking about creating event assets, and some of the
key items you're going to need to prepare for your event.
One of the first items you'll want to start with is creating the outline of your event
agenda. You can do this easily in another portion of this course, so go ahead and start
there. When putting together the final details of the agenda, just be sure to include any
speakers that you've coordinated to present. Their available schedules, dates and times
may shift the agenda slightly, but if the content that they're going to be providing is
going to provide an awesome value for your attendees, you'll want to make sure that
you can shift around a few items to keep that time there.
Be sure to ask them if they're going to be needing a slide presentation, or any event
materials that they're planning to bring or need printed beforehand. This will save you
some stress when they do arrive. If there is an item that needs to happen and you're at
an event location, be sure to check with the front desk to see if they can do the printing
for you.
Along with the agenda, you'll want to take note any times that you'll need handouts,
prints, action steps and training times, and any necessary breaks. Once this has been
finalized, be sure to print a few copies for on-site reference. This will be great for the
expert and any of your staff that will be working the event, so that they can all be on the
same page with what the expectations are.
I'm going to share with you a simple spreadsheet that you can use to track your event

any logistical items that have a cost associated with them.
This spreadsheet is just a simple way for you to track those items. What you're going to
want to do is keep the payment source on the left hand side, the vendor here, and then
a description of the item can really help in understanding and moving forward if you
choose to do the event again, if this is something you need to include for those items.
Then you'll just plug in the number here. This can easily be changed and then it's all
added at the very bottom.
The purpose of this is really just to make sure that you are spending the amount of
money that you want for the event, and if it needs to be adjusted, you can always do
that. At the very bottom, you'll want to enter the amount of attendees that you have

The next thing on the list is printed materials. This will be one easy way to deliver

For example, if you want to make sure your attendees are not promoting or soliciting
their business during the event, you may want to include this. This is just a document

You'll also want to include printing any tools and worksheets for the event. You can
hand these out in multiple ways, but we typically do this in one of the following. The
first one is in a binder. You can preset all of the items in a neat binder or folder, with a
cover that's branded to your particular business, or industry. Then what you'll want to
do is just have those items there, and have the speaker or the expert reference them
when they're ready to utilize them. Another way to get those items to them, is to hand
pass the documents This would be collated at the back of the room, and when you're
ready to discuss the item, either hand it out yourself, or have someone on your team
hand them out.
The decision between the two should be strategic. If you are okay with the attendees
seeing the items beforehand, a binder or folder can be an awesome option that can cut
down time of handouts during the event. However, if the item needs to eliminate the
item of surprise, it needs to have an introduction or it may cause confusion. It may be
best to hand it out when you and your attendees are ready. In those printed materials,
you'll also want to include name badges, place cards or table tents, wifi tapes if you are
planning to include internet during the event, and any signage for the event.
You'll want to make sure you receive all speaker slides before the event, if possible. This
will give you a chance to review their topics and align the content of the event
